data.epi_content:
The tobacco and cigars in the Empire were simply dried leaves smoked without any harmful chemicals from factories—an environment that environmentalists would love, enjoying plants in their natural form—which made their scent incredibly strong.

Even when heavily coated with chocolate or fruit scents, the harsh smoke remained just as potent.

The Emperor of the Empire and even the lords who trembled at his commands were such romantic environmentalists who loved nature so deeply that they adored these lovely tobacco leaves far more than their lowly subjects who destroyed nature.

So perhaps the only happiness for those who call themselves nobles, despite their fallen houses, is being able to puff away at tobacco and send smoke billowing upward.

"Erica, the room is filled with smoke. How about some ventilation?"

"What does it matter? Want to try one? This time I infused it with the tea scent you brought me."

With just a flick of Evan's finger, the air in the room began to circulate instantly.

Then, he gathered all the cigarette smoke in one place, opened the veranda door, and threw it outside.

As if it were something harmful to the body.

Well, I suppose it is harmful, but couldn't healing magic fix that somehow?

If cancer developed in the lungs, couldn't you just cut out the lungs and use healing magic to regrow them?

I think that's what happened with that nameless organ that was torn out.

"I tried it once because you liked it, but I don't enjoy it that much."

"Really?"

I took out a cigarette, withdrew the hand that was about to offer it to Evan, and scratched the back of my head, feeling slightly embarrassed.

Not that the embarrassment would go away by doing so.

"How many days has it been since you returned?"

"About two weeks, I think. Why?"

Evan is busy cleaning the room I've thoroughly messed up.

He's also using a spell like a washing machine in mid-air to clean the bloodstains on the towel.

"It's about time Vivian would come looking for me. At least, that's what happened the first time."

I clutch my slightly stinging wrist.

This time I just had nothing to do, so I clawed at my wrist with my fingernails.

It was partly out of boredom, but punishing vile humans is the proper attitude a lady of House Mecklenburg should naturally have.

"Back then, after the family completely collapsed and both Father and my brother died, when I was thinking about dying too, Vivian came and comforted me."

"Comforted you?"

"Wouldn't providing appropriate drinks and cookies to someone who looks like they're about to die give you quite a satisfying feeling?"

"But would Vivian think of you that way..."

Evan stopped mid-sentence.

Come to think of it, Vivian has always wanted me not to die.

Yes, she wanted me not to die.

Putting happiness aside first, and postponing all mental aspects like pride that makes life sustainable or motivation for living, as if it didn't matter if the mind died as long as the body remained alive.

Perhaps if you saw your father dangling before your eyes with his tongue sticking out, you might think that staying alive comes first.

But even that father committed physical suicide because his mind had died, so there's no bigger fool than someone who only remembers what they saw but learns no lessons from what happened.

"She would. Vivian would probably be terribly sad if you died."

"Yes, she's that kind of person. She'd be sad if I died, but she wouldn't do crazy things like you, trying to somehow save someone who's already dead."

At those words, Evan walks toward me with a smile.

"Do you dislike that?"
data.epi_content2:
"No, I love it so much it drives me crazy."

When I first died bleeding profusely, when my head separated from my body the second time allowing me to swim in the blue sea called sky, when a bullet stirred my head into mush, when Miss Erica Mecklenburg became Mecklenburg barbecue.

And even when I was captured by the Crown Prince and abused and mistreated by the Blacks.

"I love it so much it drives me crazy. I'm so satisfied with this moment of being alive and breathing!

Sometimes I wonder if I'm alive and want to check if red blood is still flowing through my body, but I'm too happy."

The problem is, others would feel the same.

They'd be happier than anyone about being alive.

For me, it was a life with many, but worthless, chances, but for them, it's their one and only life.

It's not that important.

No.

That's right.

"I wonder if Vivian feels this kind of joy every day about being alive."

Evan didn't answer.

Well, lately I've had many moments when emotions suddenly surge up and words spill out like this.

And at such times, Evan folds his hands neatly like a pope looking at his goddess, watching me rejoicing in life and deriving his own satisfaction.

Whether it's love, fraternity, or just simple altruism, nobody knows.

Only Evan knows.

"That's hard to say.

But I pushed past Vivian who was blocking me and finally returned to where you were alive, and though it's hard to say I've saved you yet, I'm in the process of saving you."

"What's your standard for having saved someone?"

"The death of the Emperor and Crown Prince. The neutralization of Vivian and those once called heroes. And the deaths of roughly a few million people."

"When you put it that way, it makes me sound like some disaster god."

"If you want to live a life without interference, a satisfying and proud life, you need to cut through that much.

Your unfortunate fate is entangled with many misfortunes in complex ways.

In the end, the best way to deal with those threads is to cut them or burn them."

Evan suddenly seems to have complicated feelings and reaches out to me.

I handed him the tea-scented cigarette I had in my mouth.

After taking one puff, he says it's still not to his taste and hands it back to me.

"Vivian will become a court magician after some time, perhaps standing alone.

And she'll make other magicians look like fools who wouldn't dare say they're comparable to her.

She's just that exceptional."

Evan seemed to have some love-hate relationship, or familiar hostility, toward Vivian.

Sometimes he would tell stories about Vivian and the people around her.

He said that during the time when Evan was active, those called heroes on the Empire's side would have all been killed long ago if it weren't for Vivian.

"So what, are you saying you're going to kill Vivian?"

I flicked off the long ash that had accumulated.

"That depends on Vivian."

Though he says that, Evan seemed to have made up his mind.

I don't know what happened between Evan and Vivian after I died, what they went through.

I never heard about it, and I didn't want to ask.

I only recall a few stories that reached me indirectly, that they had a falling out, that Evan hates Vivian.

Just those thoughts come to mind.
data.epi_content3:
"Is it really necessary to go that far?"

I don't particularly like extremes.

Unlike those who couldn't bear the stress anymore and caused disturbances downtown, stealing police guns and ammunition and taking hostages, I just shut myself in my room and isolated myself.

Occasionally, when it became too painful, I would go to the park, watch children playing, and blow away my distress with smoke.

I don't think I ever did anything beyond that. Probably.

Of course, the park was a non-smoking area, but that was old news—as long as you didn't cause a disturbance, no one really cared what you did within reason.

"I just met with Vivian.

Oh, I mean the gentleman from House Strelitz.

Vivian said she feels so bad about fighting with you that she wants to apologize.

She's planning to bake cookies and other things you might like."

"And?"

"Vivian thinks of you as a friend.

And she likes you quite a bit.

Just thinking about it normally, if you just stopped trying to die in front of her, she wouldn't necessarily cling to you like last time."

That's a difficult statement to respond to.

It's also quite awkward.

So instead of answering, I just let out a slight laugh.

The air I exhaled was a mix of foul tobacco smoke and fragrant tea scent.

Though the former was in much higher proportion.

"Haha."

"The Crown Prince probably won't try to bother you right now when the atmosphere is so unsettled.

Just get along with her moderately, then poison Vivian's food or blow her head off while she's sleeping."

"I told you, is it really necessary to go that far?"

To that, Evan speaks as if stating obvious common sense.

"If Vivian could only save you and two complete strangers, who do you think she would choose?"

Then he changes his words, saying, "Ah, she might choose you for two, so let's make it three people."

I couldn't answer easily.

The righteous and kind Vivian would probably choose three people whose names and faces she didn't even know, rather than me.

"The Crown Prince might lose interest and let you go, but you know well that's unlikely to happen.

He'll probably try to keep you locked up in a cage hidden deep within the Imperial Palace.

And you wouldn't want to be dragged there. So you'll either have to run away for the rest of your life or somehow throw the Empire into chaos."

Then he continued.

"But would Vivian still be on your side then?"

I heard something I didn't want to hear.

I don't think she was ever on my side.

At critical moments, for some reason, she always seemed to be on the side that tormented me.

So I just blurt out an irresponsible answer.

"I don't know."

Evan didn't say anything more to me.

As always, it feels a bit tiring.
